posture: Quiet Wednesday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, AI workforce readiness, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest in-scope material sits just outside the 24-hour window (City of Moreton Bay's Australian-first AI traffic-signal trial, publicised ~2–15 July) rather than being a new dated event.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a–§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring or out-of-window items were reviewed and excluded: City of Moreton Bay's Australian-first AI-powered traffic-signal trial at Petrie (a genuine in-scope roads/mobility signal, but publicised roughly 2–15 July and so outside the 24-hour window); the City of Vincent National Awards for Local Government wins (GreenTrack, Near Miss Pilot Program) reported by LG Focus on 20 July but stemming from the 22 June awards event, so out of window; Austroads' updated Guide to Pavement Technology Part 4 recycled-materials framework (6 March 2026); the Hawkesbury / ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial (a January 2026 development, previously excluded); and Auckland Council's AI recycling-contamination work and Harrow Council's drone-fed digital twin (January and April 2026 respectively). Paid titles (AFR/SMH/LGC/MJ) were held to headline + standfirst and not used. Today is Wednesday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. Sections are published empty per §6.
